title: practical english usage - Part 89
content:
Aren't you interested in knowing / to know whether I'm pregnant?

grammar •  108   causative and similar structures with get For have + object + infinitive (meaning ‘ order/instruct somebody to do something’ ), ►     109.1.

See if you can get the car to start.

I can't get that child to go to bed. Get Penny to help us if you can.

difficulty.

‘ persuade somebody/something to do something’ : there is often an idea of Get + object + infinitive means ‘ make somebody/something do something' or 2 causative:      Get   Penny       to   help     us.

Once we got the heater going the car started to warm up.

Don't get him  talking about his illnesses.

Get + object + .. .ing means 'make somebody/something start.. .ing’ .

1  causative:      Don't    get    him     talking.

108 causative       a nd   sim  ilar   structu     res   with    get For other structures with make and the difference between make and do, ►    435.

You have made me a happy man. (<    Y  have made me he a happy man.) We do not use make. .. be in this structure.

The rain made the grass wet. (     The rain made wet- the grass.) She made everybody welcome.

(►   10).

We   can talk about an effect or change with make + object + adjective/noun 3 with    object     + object     complement:        make      people        welcome,          etc She had to shout to make herself heard.

I don’ t speak good Japanese, but I can make myself understood.

participle. The  structure is common with understood and heard.

In a few cases make can be followed by myself, yourself, etc, and a past 2  make      oneself        understood,            etc She was made to repeat the whole story.

In passive structures the infinitive with to is used.

I can’t make the washing machine work, (     I can't make work the Note that the infinitive must follow the object.

I made her cry. (     / made her to cry. on / made-hcr crying.) After make + object, we  use the infinitive without to (►   91).

1 object     + infinitive 107 causative       structures       with    make -ing                                                         Section Infinitives,        forms     and   Past   Participles       after    Nouns,    Verbs,   etc         9 grammar «109     causative and similar structures with have We had a tax inspector come to the office yesterday.

/ had a very strange thing happen to me when I was fourteen.

In the structure have + object + infinitive/.. .ing, have can mean 'experience’ .

3 experience:       have      something           happen/happening Get is used in a similar structure, ►     108.3.

If you don't get out of my house I'll have you arrested.

I'm going to have my hair cut this afternoon. (= I want my  hair to be cut.) I must have my watch repaired. (= I want my  watch to be repaired.) somebody else’ . The  past participle has a passive meaning.

Have + object + past participle can mean ‘ cause something to be done by 2 causative:      have      something           done something’ ), ►     108.2.

For get + object + infinitive (meaning 'persuade somebody/something to do He had us laughing all through the meal.

something’  (BrE and AmE).

The  structure with an -ing form can mean 'cause somebody to be doing The manager had everybody fill out a form.

I'm ready to see Mr Smith. Have him  come in, please.

mostly used in American English, to talk about giving instructions or orders.

Have + object + infinitive can mean ‘ cause somebody to do something'. This is 1  causative:      have      somebody           do/doing         something object + past participle.

Have can be followed by object + infinitive (without to), object + -ing, and 109 causative       and   similar     structures       with    have After you've got the children dressed, can you make the beds?

It will take me another hour to get the washing done.

something.

We   can also use get + object + past participle to talk about completing work on 5  Get   the    children         dressed.

This idea is more often expressed with have (e.g.  We had our roof blown off).

We got our roof blown off in the storm last week.
